title: lanthorus pentapetalus
intro: Lanthorus pentapetalus, also known as Lathyrus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the pea family. It is a perennial legume that is native to the Old World and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains a toxin called Lathyrine, which has been reported to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Lanthorus pentapetalus include tea, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
